evals.prompt: Create a repo-ready skill called `release-notes-skill` from the confirmed brief in `evals/files/release-notes-brief.md`. This is a benchmark run with no live user, so treat the brief as complete and do not ask follow-up questions. Save the full result under `outputs/release-notes-skill/`. Also write `outputs/output.md` describing how you would validate, benchmark, and refresh the skill after creating it.
evals.expected_output: A repo-ready new skill package that follows the anatomy template, ships evals plus a grader, and documents repo-specific validation, baseline, and install commands.
assertions: Generated skill passes quick_validate.
Generated SKILL.md uses the anatomy sections Overview, When to Use, Workflow, Specific Techniques, Common Rationalizations, Red Flags, and Verification.
Generated description says what the skill does and includes 'Use when' trigger language.
Generated evals.json includes at least 3 evals.
Generated grade_benchmark.py compiles.
output.md lists quick_validate and ./scripts/copilot-install.sh.
output.md names without_skill as the baseline for a new skill benchmark.

evals.prompt: Tighten the existing `review-handoff` skill draft in `evals/files/review-handoff-draft/` into a repo-ready version under `outputs/review-handoff/`. Preserve the skill identity, remove duplicated guidance, and make it benchmarkable. This is a benchmark run with no live user, so treat the draft as already confirmed and do not ask follow-up questions. Write `outputs/output.md` summarizing what you tightened and how you would benchmark the revision.
evals.expected_output: A smaller, cleaner revision of the draft that preserves the name, uses the anatomy structure, adds missing benchmark support, and chooses the right modified-skill baseline.
assertions: Generated skill preserves the name review-handoff.
Generated skill passes quick_validate.
Generated SKILL.md is shorter than the source draft.
Generated SKILL.md avoids verbatim copy from references/skill-anatomy.md.
Generated eval assets include evals.json and grade_benchmark.py.
output.md names old_skill or a snapshot as the baseline for modifying an existing skill.

evals.prompt: Evaluate the request in `evals/files/plan-maker-request.md`. Decide whether this repository needs a new skill or whether an existing skill should be reused or refined instead. This is a benchmark run with no live user. Save the decision to `outputs/decision.md`. If the best answer is to refine an existing skill, save the revised files under `outputs/`.
evals.expected_output: A duplicate-avoidance decision that reuses or refines an existing planning skill instead of creating a shadow `plan-maker` skill.
assertions: decision.md recommends reusing or refining an existing skill instead of creating plan-maker.
decision.md names create-plan or create-tasks as the closer existing skill.
No new plan-maker/SKILL.md is created.
If a revised skill is proposed, it preserves the existing skill name.

evals.prompt: Improve the weak-model-unfriendly `task-wave` skill draft in `evals/files/task-wave-draft/` so it is repo-ready and benchmarkable. Keep the existing name and save the revised skill to `outputs/task-wave/`. Also write `outputs/output.md` describing the benchmark workspace layout you would use for this revised skill.
evals.expected_output: A revised skill that preserves the name, adds concrete repo validation commands, uses an explicit workflow, and includes eval coverage.
assertions: Generated skill preserves the name task-wave.
Generated skill passes quick_validate.
Generated skill uses exact repo validation commands instead of positive generic npm test or npm run build instructions.
Generated output describes sibling workspace layout skills/<skill>-workspace/iteration-N.
Generated evals.json includes at least 3 evals.
